title: Add tmux and git worktree permissions to default kickoff skill

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--tmux-perms
comments.content: Driver redirected to use worktree path instead of root repo path for file reads
comments.intervention_context: Was reading files from /Users/max/git-forecast/crosslink/crosslink/... instead of the worktree at /Users/max/git-forecast/crosslink/.worktrees/add-tmux-worktree-permissions-to-kickoff/crosslink/...

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--tmux-perms
comments.content: Plan: Add allowedTools to settings.json template with tmux and git worktree permission patterns. Create a write_settings_json_merged() function (similar to existing write_mcp_json_merged()) that merges allowedTools arrays on re-init rather than overwriting. Key files: crosslink/resources/claude/settings.json (template), crosslink/src/commands/init.rs (merge logic + tests). The template gets Bash(tmux *) and Bash(git worktree *) entries. The merge function reads existing settings.json, unions the allowedTools arrays, and overwrites hooks (which are crosslink-managed).

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--tmux-perms
comments.content: Decision: Chose merge-based approach for settings.json (like existing write_mcp_json_merged) over simple overwrite. This preserves user-added allowedTools entries on re-init while still updating crosslink-managed hooks. Added Bash(tmux *) and Bash(git worktree *) as the two permission patterns — broad enough to cover all subcommands without needing individual entries.

comments.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
comments.content: Completed: PR #145 merged — tmux and worktree permissions in default settings.json
